items.title: 'Attention-seeking' man allegedly targeted police, defence in 'cybercrime spree'

children.content: A 22-year-old Adelaide man who allegedly went on a "cybercrime spree", targeting several government departments and a courthouse, will remain behind bars until at the least the end of the year.

items.title: 'No-one's committed murder here': Port CEO urges change to tribunal process

children.content: Port Adelaide's chief executive has slammed the AFL Tribunal process, labelling it "too legalistic" and "too adversarial".

image.altText: A man is a suit is frowning as he looks toward his left side.

items.title: Uncovered ballots prompt new count in key SA seat won by One Nation

children.content: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has uncovered more than 600 ballot papers that were not counted in the recent state election, including dozens from the closely contested seat of Narungga on the Yorke Peninsula.

image.altText: A composite image of two women with blonde hair

items.title: SA government has 'assurances' from LIV Golf as course upgrade under fresh scrutiny

children.content: The South Australian government has reaffirmed its commitment to a $45 million upgrade of the North Adelaide public golf course to host LIV Golf in 2028, and has "assurances" about the tour's future.

articles.title: Is LIV Golf dead? Not yet, but the vultures are circling

children.content: Rumours of LIV Golf's immediate demise look wide of the mark, but the smoke is only growing thicker as denials from the league's CEO help paint a bleak picture.

image.altText: Cameron Smith at a LIV Golf press conference

articles.title: LIV Golf season to continue 'full throttle' amid funding speculation

children.content: LIV Golf will run as planned in 2026 according to the league's CEO, as media reports suggest its funding from Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund may soon end.

image.altText: Golf executive standing on the first tee, looking into the distance

items.title: SA government apologises to prisoner denied abortion medication

children.content: The South Australian ombudsman calls for changes to medical monitoring at the Adelaide Women's Prison after a female prisoner was denied a medical abortion, which the health minister says is unacceptable.

image.altText: A building surrounded by a fence and trees

items.title: Australia's rarest reptile found after being considered extinct

children.content: Not found anywhere else on Earth, a skink considered functionally extinct has been found in a national park in far west NSW.

image.altText: A skink on a large rock

items.title: Teen admits creating deepfakes in Australian-first prosecution

children.content: An Adelaide teenager who was the first person charged under new nationwide laws aimed at tackling adult deepfake pornography has pleaded guilty to two counts of creating or altering sexual material without consent.

image.altText: William Yeates is wearing a white shirt and looking over his shoulder

items.title: AFL 'firestorm' taking heavy toll on players, Saints coach says

children.content: St Kilda coach Ross Lyon says the damage from a "firestorm" around the AFL Tribunal is weighing heavily as his club signals it may appeal against Lance Collard's suspension.

image.altText: A St Kilda AFL coach stands on the ground looking downfield ahead of a match.

items.title: The outback jobs disappearing as fuel costs hit tourism

children.content: From roadhouses to stations, outback workers are losing their jobs as tourists stay away. Skyrocketing fuel prices, as well as concerns around the availability of fuel in remote locations, are causing a 76 per cent loss of revenue for tourist-dependent businesses.

image.altText: A young woman bottle feeds a calf and a lamb.

items.title: Blow to cartography in Australia as The Map Shop prepares to close

children.content: One of Australia's few remaining cartography shops is about to close in Adelaide, prompting questions about where people can buy the critical documents, particularly those travelling in remote areas where mobile coverage is limited.

image.altText: Man stands in front of shelf of maps

items.title: The cause of the Viva Energy oil refinery fire under investigation. Here's what we know

children.content: Firefighters have managed to extinguish a blaze at one of Australia's major oil refineries. Here's what we know so far about the cause of the fire — and the possible impact on fuel prices.

image.altText: fire and smoke pictured above the Geelong refinery in the evening

items.title: Hanson wishes Coalition good luck, government focuses on fuel

children.content: This week in Canberra, Pauline Hanson learned imitation is the sincerest form of flattery, the defence budget was on the agenda and a fire threw the fuel crisis into sharp relief.

items.title: 'Calculated' theft of $12,000 worth of fuel prompts arrest

children.content: A 36-year-old man has been charged with theft amid an investigation into as many as 20 petrol drive-offs, which police say involved an SUV that contained a tank used to carry stolen diesel.

items.title: New data reveals age group spending up big

children.content: Australians spent more in March as the outbreak of the Iran war drove up petrol prices. Older Australians increased their spending at the fastest pace, CBA data shows.

items.title: Report finds Darwin Harbour biodiversity rivals that of Ningaloo Reef

items.title: Farmers 'running on empty' welcome arrival of diesel

children.content: A massive crude oil tanker laden with diesel has docked in the Port of Newcastle to replenish regional New South Wales supplies, as the Middle East unrest continues.

items.title: Smoky Sydney as 5,000 hectares burned in hazard reduction blitz

children.content: Smoky conditions across Greater Sydney are tipped to ease tomorrow as the wind picks up, after firefighters had a productive week burning hazards around Sydney's fringes.

items.title: Queensland massage therapist faces 18 sexual assault charges

children.content: A Queensland massage therapist arrested in February over the alleged sexual assault of four clients is facing 11 fresh charges after another four female clients came forward.

items.title: Man, 53, charged over seizure of more than 350 cannabis plants

children.content: A 53-year-old Kalgoorlie man is released on bail after the seizure of more than 350 cannabis plants from a property WA Police allege had been converted for the purpose of growing hydroponic cannabis.

items.title: 'Snacks' ordered for alleged hit men's escape after underworld killing, court hears

items.title: Unemployment rate remains at 4.3pc in 'calm before the storm'

children.content: Australia's unemployment rate remained at 4.3 per cent in March, but economists say cracks could start appearing in coming months.

items.title: Liberty Bell Bay workers told to take leave without pay or be sacked

children.content: About 175 workers at Australia's only manganese smelter, Liberty Bell Bay, have been told they will be laid off at the end of next week unless they take leave without pay.

items.title: 2032 Olympic rowing 'will be in Rockhampton': Mander

children.content: The Queensland sports minister has once again emphasised that the Brisbane 2032 Games will be an Olympics for the whole state, saying there are no alternatives planned for the contentious proposed rowing venue in Rockhampton.
